bug_report.summary: Preview click on linked badge/link highlights/selects Markdown source instead of acting like a link click (and selection mapping changes)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file.
Paste: [![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-2025--10--13-darkgreen)](https://pandoc.org/)
Open the Markdown preview.
Click the badge (or the pandoc.org link) in the preview.
expected_behaviors: Clicking a link in the preview should behave like a link click (open/navigate), or at minimum remain consistent with previous behavior and not unexpectedly select source text.
If the app supports click-to-source mapping, the selected range should consistently correspond to the clicked element (image+link), not just a partial fragment such as only the destination URL.
actual_behaviors: After change, clicking in the preview results in source highlighting/selection in the editor (e.g., highlighting “(https://pandoc.org/)” next to the rendered badge), indicating the click is being captured for editor selection rather than straightforward link activation, and the mapped selection range differs from before.

bug_report.summary: Workspace tree selection/highlight differs between builds after dismissing the update dialog (selection jumps from “Helpful Links” to “Split View”)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch Zettlr with the tutorial workspace visible (e.g., Zettlr Tutorial).
Ensure both “Helpful Links” and “Split View” are present/open as shown.
Trigger the “Update available” modal dialog (or wait for it to appear).
Click “Not now” to dismiss the dialog.
Observe which file is highlighted/selected in the workspace tree.
expected_behaviors: Dismissing the update dialog should not change the currently selected/highlighted item in the workspace tree; selection should remain stable.
actual_behaviors: After the code change, the highlighted/selected item in the workspace tree is different than before (it highlights “Split View” instead of “Helpful Links”), implying selection changes unexpectedly.

bug_report.summary: Preview heading marker rendering differs between builds (green “#” next to headings appears shifted/clipped)
steps_to_reproduce: Open a document in split/preview view that contains H1 headings (e.g., “Split View” and “Helpful Links”).
Look at the left side of the H1 headings in the preview panes where the green “#” heading marker is rendered.
expected_behaviors: Heading markers should render consistently (same alignment/shape) across builds.
actual_behaviors: After the code change, the heading marker rendering near H1s differs from before (marker position/shape appears altered in both the top and bottom preview panes).

bug_report.summary: Autolink/hover-preview truncates URL destination at the first space (query loses trailing text)
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown file.
Type a URL on its own line that contains a space in the query, e.g. `https://duckduckgo.com/?q=hello world`.
Open the Markdown preview (or use split view).
Hover the rendered link (or otherwise trigger the link preview/tooltip).
expected_behaviors: The rendered link destination (and any tooltip/hover preview) should include the full URL including the space segment (i.e., it should resolve to `...?q=hello world`, consistent with the refactor intent to allow spaces in destinations).
actual_behaviors: The hover preview/tooltip shows the destination truncated at the space (e.g., `https://duckduckgo.com/?q=hello`), indicating the space is still terminating the parsed URL.

bug_report.summary: Active document unexpectedly switches while editing (new file input ends up in “Helpful Links”), and right-side preview panes reshuffle displayed documents
steps_to_reproduce: Open Zettlr with split view enabled (two preview panes on the right) and the tutorial workspace available (e.g., “Split View” and “Helpful Links” visible).
Create a new Markdown file (File → New file… → Markdown → Save).
Click into the editor area of the new file and type `https://duckduckgo.com/?q=hello world`.
Observe the workspace tree selection and the content rendered in the right preview panes.
expected_behaviors: The newly created file remains the active/selected document while typing.
Typed content is applied only to the new file.
Preview panes should not spontaneously swap which documents they display just from focusing/typing in the editor.
actual_behaviors: After the change, selection switches to another document (e.g., “Helpful Links”) while typing/continuing the scenario.
The typed URL/link content appears in the “Helpful Links” preview/context instead of only in the new file.
The right-side preview panes/tabs reshuffle which document is shown (blank/new-file content vs other documents moving between panes).

bug_report.summary: Open Link opens URL-like text from link label instead of the Markdown link destination
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file in Zettlr.
Enter: [Example: www.example.com/my-path](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986)
Right-click the rendered link text (the label) in the editor/preview.
Choose “Open link” from the context menu.
expected_behaviors: Browser should open the actual link destination: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986.
actual_behaviors: Browser opens www.example.com/my-path (the URL-like substring from the link label) instead of the link destination.

bug_report.summary: Word/character count changes unexpectedly after typing a Markdown link with parentheses + title (stats appear inflated vs before change)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file.
Enter: `[Wikipedia test](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Function_(mathematics) "Math article")`
Observe the word/character counters (top-right header stats and the bottom status bar for the active document).
expected_behaviors: Document statistics (word and character count) should be consistent for the same input across builds; link parsing refactor should not change basic stats computation unless explicitly intended.
If stats are meant to count rendered/visible text only, they should not include non-visible parts like link titles/URL destinations.
actual_behaviors: Before change, stats show ~2 words / 14 characters (matching visible text “Wikipedia test”).
After change, stats show ~4 words / 26 characters, despite the preview still displaying only “Wikipedia test”.

bug_report.summary: Reference-style link renders as raw Markdown (“[Search ref][sref]”) instead of rendered link text (“Search ref”)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file

Enter the following content:

[Search ref][sref]

[sref]: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?search=hello world "Ref title"
View the rendered output for the note (in the pane showing rendered Markdown / rendering preview mode)
expected_behaviors: The rendered output shows a clickable link with the text “Search ref” (reference label [sref] should not be shown as literal markup).
actual_behaviors: The rendered output shows the raw reference-style link markup “[Search ref][sref]” instead of rendering it as a link text only.

bug_report.summary: Markdown preview renders edited link incorrectly: URL shown as literal text on new line and brackets appear misplaced
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file.
Type: `[Hello, world!](https://example.com/)`
Insert an additional `[` at the very beginning of the line (so it starts with `[[`).
Press End and type: ` still typing` (so the line ends with that text).
Click the eye (Preview) icon to open Markdown preview/rendering for the file.
expected_behaviors: Preview should render a leading literal `[` followed by a normal link with text `Hello, world!`, then the trailing text ` still typing` (i.e., no destination URL displayed as plain text, and no bracket re-ordering).
Rendering should remain stable/consistent after the partial edit.
actual_behaviors: After the change, the preview shows a malformed representation where the URL `(https://example.com/)` is visible as literal text on a separate line and the bracket placement/line structure differs (e.g., text appears as `[[Hello, world! still typing]` with the URL below), indicating the parser is misinterpreting the edited link.
